description_long: This object is a medieval-style catapult handcart constructed primarily from dark, weathered wood reinforced with metal bands and bolts. It features four large, spoked wheels with metallic rims, providing stability and mobility. The main frame supports a robust throwing arm, tensioned by ropes and a winch mechanism, with a large metal bowl or bucket at the end for holding projectiles. The front of the cart is dominated by a horizontal wooden bar, likely used for pushing or pulling the cart. Metal hardware, such as axles, brackets, and fasteners, are visible throughout, adding to the structural integrity. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed mechanical components and a rugged, functional appearance.
description: A medieval-style wooden catapult handcart with four metal-rimmed wheels, a large throwing arm, and a metal projectile bowl. Reinforced with metal hardware and ropes, it has a rugged, functional look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide horizontal handlebar, the metal bowl centered above the wheels, and the sturdy wooden frame.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full length of the throwing arm, the tension ropes, and the arrangement of the four wheels along the frame.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the back of the metal bowl, the cross-braced wooden frame, and the rear wheels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the throwing arm, ropes, and wheel placement, mirroring the other side.
